title: Lesson 14: Navigation Bars (Building Menus with HTML)

content.explanation:
🧭 What is Navigation?

Every website you use has a navigation menu — the bar at the top that lets you move between pages.

Navigation is built using a combination of:

  • <ul> (list container)
  • <li> (menu items)
  • <a> (links)

🌐 Why Navigation is Important

A website without navigation is like a book with no table of contents — users get lost quickly.

Good navigation = better user experience + professional websites.
content.syntax_breakdown:
🧪 Basic Navigation Structure

<ul>
  <li><a href="#home">Home</a></li>
  <li><a href="#about">About</a></li>
  <li><a href="#contact">Contact</a></li>
</ul>

🧠 Key Idea

👉 Lists organize items
👉 Links make them clickable
👉 Together they form navigation menus

📌 Real Website Example

Most websites (Google, YouTube, Facebook) use navigation bars built using this exact structure — later styled with CSS.

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My Website

Welcome to my homepage.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Build a navigation menu

1. Below the paragraph, create a <ul>
2. Add 3 menu items using <li>
3. Inside each <li>, add an <a> link:
   - Home (#home)
   - About (#about)
   - Contact (#contact)

👉 Run your code and see a working menu structure
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Example:
  • Home
    • Remember: link goes inside list item

quiz_engine.question: What is the correct structure for building a navigation menu in HTML?
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 Navigation menus are built using lists (<ul>, <li>) combined with links (<a>) to create structured clickable menus.

takeaways: Navigation helps users move through a website
Menus are built using lists and links together
